title: Integer / integer truncates to integer, silently corrupting hand-rolled averages and ratios
rule: In SQL, integer / integer performs integer division (truncating toward zero), so SUM(x)/COUNT(x) or count_a/count_b drops the fraction before any ROUND can help. Force float/decimal division by casting one operand (x*1.0, CAST(x AS REAL), or ::numeric); for a plain mean, just use AVG(), which already returns a real.
triggers: SUM divided by COUNT returns integer
ratio always rounds down to whole number
